reviewer: agent build + matrix pass (revision 1) — headless Chrome, real inference + mobile/desktop screenshots read

rubric.evidence: Headless Chrome (own driver, WASM no-GPU): first-visit Download (~1272 MB) → real output "supports" 99.7% in 294 ms on WASM for claim "Remote work improves productivity." + supporting text. probs SUPPORTS 99.7% / REFUTES 0.3% sum to 100%. console + network clean. onnxruntime-web WASM successfully created a session from the 1.27 GB int8 XLM-RoBERTa model. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/stance-detection/desktop.png, mobile.png
rubric.notes: Confirmed the browser (not just Node) loads and runs the large model; the 1.27 GB download is honest and cached.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): io-shape-semantic=manual. AGENT VERDICT: 2-class softmax (SUPPORTS + REFUTES) summing to 1.00, target-conditioned. Node runnability pre-check (transformers.js@3.7.5) scored 8/8 on claim+evidence pairs (support→SUPPORTS 0.99-1.00, refute→REFUTES 0.88-0.99) using the verified `claim </s></s> text` single-string formulation.
rubric.notes: The pipeline text_pair path returns a degenerate constant (0.6531/0.3469) for this checkpoint; the single-string join is the verified correct input — documented in worker.js + the page's How-the-API-works.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (Factiverse/factiverse_stance_detection_ort_quantized linked), declares-task=pass (text-classification), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (worker.js). ONNX-at-root handled with subfolder:"".

conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader). AGENT VERDICT: cached second visit auto-inits to ready with NO Download button and a "Clear cached model" control present (verified in the same headless browser session after the 1.27 GB first download). No silent large re-download.

conformance: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass (.err status + Retry via shared loader). honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback=manual — WASM path runs with no GPU; the at-a-glance prominently states the ~1.27 GB one-time download so the visitor consents before transferring.

conformance: controls-operable=manual. AGENT VERDICT: claim input + text input drive as-you-type scoring (debounced), sample chips set claim/text, the flip button scores the text against the claim and its negation, practical board maps a batch, wild gauntlet + adversarial editor run — all real, labelled, keyboard-operable.

conformance: no-fake-output=manual. AGENT VERDICT: every stance score comes from the on-device worker run; the "unclear" band is an honest derivation from a near-50/50 softmax margin (the model has no separate neutral class), stated in the UI; no canned results.
conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Loader status is role=status aria-live=polite; claim/text inputs + buttons labelled.

conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. AGENT VERDICT: desktop 1280×800 + mobile 360×740 screenshots read — no horizontal overflow, legible text, at-a-glance table + stance meter render, model-id link wraps. Because the model is a heavy ~1.27 GB download, the page flags 'desktop recommended' honestly while remaining functional on mobile (the auto-init/explicit-download policy lets the user choose). screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/stance-detection/

conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. Inference runs off the main thread (Web Worker) and as-you-type scoring is debounced (400 ms); a single forward pass is ~294 ms on WASM (measured), so typing stays responsive; batch scoring is one padded forward pass.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference runs off the main thread via a Web Worker; the control UI stays responsive and as-you-type scoring is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread = pass; worker.js runs pipeline() with subfolder:""

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Identify slow JS impacting INP; keep interaction handlers light.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the input handlers only schedule a debounced worker call; no synchronous model work on the main thread despite the large model.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: debounce(score, 400) + worker offload; measured 294 ms inference stays off the main thread

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0; auto-fit minmax() grids; logical properties — no fixed widths that overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls-grid/pair-grid/board use repeat(auto-fit,minmax()); prob-bar + arg .msg use min-inline-size:0 / flex:1 1 220px; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check: desktop+mobile no horizontal overflow; screenshots reports/responsive/screens/stance-detection/

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference state in a role=status aria-live region; every control labelled + keyboard operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader status is role=status aria-live=polite; claim (text input) + text (textarea) + buttons all labelled; sample chips are real <button type=button>.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate visible labels with input/textarea controls; keep sample chips focusable buttons.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — every input has an associated <label>; claim + text are labelled; chips are <button type=button> that set claim/text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless run drove chips + inputs + batch/flip buttons; conformance controls checks pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared design system, invariant 6).

openQuestions: The 1.27 GB download is heavy for mobile/constrained devices; the page degrades honestly (user-initiated download, 'desktop recommended') but is there a smaller stance checkpoint worth exporting to ONNX to complement this one?

The model has only SUPPORTS/REFUTES (no neutral class); the 'unclear' band is derived from a near-50/50 margin. Is that derivation communicated clearly enough that users don't read it as a real third class?
The checkpoint's text_pair path is degenerate; the single-string `</s></s>` join is verified but undocumented upstream — worth flagging to the model authors.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a WebGPU fast-path option for stance-detection (dtype q4f16) with honest WASM fallback, to cut the ~294 ms/forward-pass latency for the argument-mapping batch workload.

summary: Revision-1 agent build + matrix pass for stance-detection (text-classification, wasm): a dedicated XLM-RoBERTa stance/fact-checking head (SUPPORTS/REFUTES, target-conditioned) — NOT relabelled NLI or sentiment. Real on-device inference verified in headless Chrome ("supports" 99.7%, 294 ms WASM, probs sum to 1.00; Node pre-check 8/8 accuracy), the 1.27 GB int8 model confirmed loadable in onnxruntime-web WASM, cached auto-init confirmed (no Load button, Clear-cache present), mobile+desktop screenshots read (no overflow, legible, 'desktop recommended' for the heavy download), console+network clean. All rubric dimensions evidenced; support flipped to ok.
